methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $114M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Speedata is a Series B chip design company founded by Jonathan Friedmann and Rami Dahan. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 6 private-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors (Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, DEEPX, and one additional peer), alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The target has raised $114M to date.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using $114M in total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $217M. Peer-median methodology anchors to the $1.0B median valuation across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data for the target. 

The triangulated range spans $184M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $608M. This wide band reflects material uncertainty around capital efficiency, technology differentiation, and near-term commercialization pathway—typical for pre-revenue or early-revenue semiconductor ventures. The peer set shows significant dispersion, with earlier-stage companies commanding substantial valuations (Etched at $5.0B, d-Matrix at $2.0B), while later-stage DEEPX trades at $529M, suggesting valuation is not purely stage-driven. Additional clarity on Speedata's revenue trajectory and architectural differentiation would materially narrow this range.
